title: ulmus scabra subf. purpurea
intro: Ulmus scabra subf. purpurea, also known as the Japanese Elm or Purple-leaved Elm, is a subspecies of the Japanese Elm tree (Ulmus scabra), native to East Asia. This deciduous tree is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds in its bark and leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Ulmus scabra subf. purpurea include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as provide protection against oxidative stress and cellular damage. In traditional herbal medicine, the bark and leaves of this tree are often prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
